description: Detects common security weaknesses that constitute failure of 'reasonable security safeguards' under DPDP Act §8 and IT Act §43A

patterns.name: Hardcoded password or secret
patterns.description: Literal string assigned to a variable that looks like a credential. Must use environment variables or a secrets manager.

patterns.name: Private key block in source file
patterns.description: PEM-encoded private key embedded directly in source code.

patterns.name: MD5 hash applied to password
patterns.description: MD5 is not acceptable for password hashing — it is too fast and unsalted hashes are trivially crackable. Use bcrypt, argon2id, or scrypt.

patterns.name: SHA-1 hash applied to password
patterns.description: SHA-1 alone is not acceptable for password hashing.

patterns.name: Plain HTTP API endpoint URL
patterns.description: Hardcoded http:// URL for an API endpoint. All API calls handling personal data must use HTTPS.

patterns.name: SQL query built by string concatenation
patterns.description: SQL built by concatenating user-supplied values. Use parameterised queries instead.

patterns.name: AWS access key ID hardcoded
patterns.description: AWS access key ID (AKIA...) hardcoded in source. Must use IAM roles or environment variables.

patterns.name: Placeholder encryption key
patterns.description: Generic or trivially guessable encryption key value.
